name: tsql-step-debugger
description: Client-side, interpreter-driven step debugger for T-SQL / MSSQL (SQL Server) stored procedures, functions, and scripts — no engine-side debug support required.

tsqlDbg.adapterPath.description: Dev override: absolute path to a locally-built TsqlDbg.Adapter executable, instead of the one bundled with this extension.

tsqlDbg.defaults.stopOnEntry.description: Default for the 'stopOnEntry' launch option when your launch config (or plain F5 with no launch.json) doesn't set it: stop at frame 0's first statement before running anything. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.waitfor.description: Default for the 'waitfor' launch option: 'skip' intercepts and logs WAITFOR DELAY/TIME instead of sending it; 'honor' sends it as-is (the session genuinely blocks). An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.boost.description: Default for the 'boost' launch option: execute whole IF/WHILE subtrees as one composed batch under Continue, instead of interpreting statement by statement. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.maxConsoleRows.description: Default for the 'maxConsoleRows' launch option: row cap for Debug Console result-set rendering. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.tempTablePageSize.description: Default for the 'tempTablePageSize' launch option: rows per page when browsing the Temp Tables scope. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.displayValueChars.description: Default for the 'displayValueChars' launch option: max characters shown per variable/cell value before truncation. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.watchBudgetMs.description: Default for the 'watchBudgetMs' launch option: shared per-stop time budget for evaluating watch expressions before remaining ones show a click-to-evaluate placeholder. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.defaults.logLevel.description: Default for the 'logLevel' launch option: 'normal' shows your script's output, errors, logpoints, and commit/rollback notices; 'verbose' additionally shows the debugger's own diagnostic annotations. An explicit launch.json value always wins.

tsqlDbg.mssql.useActiveEditorConnection.description: When the active .sql file is connected in the Microsoft "SQL Server (mssql)" extension, debug against that same connection automatically (no connection picker). The first use prompts mssql to approve connection sharing. Turn this off to always use the T-SQL Debugger's own Connection Manager.

tsqlDbg.mssql.useConnectionPicker.description: When you pick a connection at launch and the Microsoft "SQL Server (mssql)" extension is installed, open its own connection picker first (its full connection list). Turn this off to lead with the T-SQL Debugger's own Connection Manager instead; mssql then appears as a secondary "Pick from SQL Server (mssql)…" entry. Only affects the manual picker, not the automatic active-file reuse above.

server.description: Server name (host, host\instance, or host,port). Leave server/database out to pick a saved connection from the Connection Manager at launch.

authType.description: 'integrated' = Windows/SSPI (default); 'sql' = SQL login (set 'sqlUser'; the password is entered in the Connection Manager and stored in VS Code SecretStorage — never in launch.json).

sqlUser.description: SQL login name when authType is 'sql'. The password is NEVER a launch-config field — it is stored in SecretStorage and passed to the adapter via a child-process environment variable.

mode.description: Debug the active .sql file as frame 0 (default), or a deployed module.

procedure.description: Two/three-part procedure name. Required when mode = procedure.

script.description: Script path (or the buffer URI for an unsaved doc). Required when mode = script.

scriptText.description: Inline script body. When set, the debugger runs this text verbatim (no file read) — used automatically for unsaved/dirty buffers so you can debug without saving.

targetsFile.description: Path to targets.json. Defaults to MSSQL_DEBUG_TARGETS env var, then ${workspaceFolder}/targets.json.

commitMode.description: On an explicit Stop/terminate you'll be asked to confirm in a modal naming the server/database — that confirmation is the authorization (no targets.json allowWrites pre-gate on the interactive VS Code surface). Declining, a 60s timeout, or any other end (disconnect, error) always rolls back instead.

stopOnEntry.description: Publish a stop at frame 0's first statement before running anything, instead of running to completion.

traceRun.description: Non-interactive trace mode: run to completion (no stops — breakpoints and stopOnEntry are ignored), streaming one line per executed statement (with variable changes, output, result sets) to the Debug Console and writing the full JSONL trace file. true = defaults; or an object: { "stepMode": "over"|"into" (default over; the Trace button uses into), "captureTempRowCounts": false, "variableCapture": "changed"|"full", "file": "explicit trace-file path" }. Rollback-always unless commitMode is 'commit' AND the run completed — then the usual commit confirmation modal.

stepMode.description: 'over' traces frame 0's statements only; 'into' steps into procedures, dynamic SQL, and triggers-eligible calls, tracing every inlined statement.

captureTempRowCounts.description: Record each live temp table/table variable's row count on every step (extra round trips).

variableCapture.description: 'changed' records only variables whose value changed since the same frame's previous statement (first stop = full baseline); 'full' records the complete snapshot every step.

file.description: Explicit trace-file path. Default: a timestamped .jsonl under the OS temp dir (tsqldbg-traces).

waitfor.description: 'skip': WAITFOR DELAY/TIME is intercepted and logged, not sent to the server. 'honor': sent as-is (the session genuinely blocks for the delay).

compatLevel.description: T-SQL parser (ScriptDom) version. 0 = auto: detect the server's version on connect and pick the matching parser (clamped to 150-170, with a warning if the server is older/newer). Or pin one: 150 (SQL 2019), 160 (SQL 2022), 170 (SQL 2025).

boost.description: Execute whole IF/WHILE subtrees as one composed batch under continue, instead of interpreting statement by statement. Off by default.

executeAs.description: Clause text emitted after 'EXECUTE AS' at session init (e.g. "USER = 'AppUser'"), REVERTed in teardown. Omit for no impersonation.

allowConsoleWrites.description: Allow the Debug Console to run DML/DDL and variable writes (not just SELECT), still inside the session transaction (rolled back at end unless commitMode:commit). Set false for a read-only console.

consoleTimeoutSec.description: Command timeout for Debug Console (REPL) evaluations, distinct from commandTimeoutSec.

maxConsoleRows.description: Row cap for Debug Console result-set rendering ("200 of N — refine your query").

tempTablePageSize.description: Rows per page when browsing the Temp Tables scope.

displayValueChars.description: Max characters shown per variable/cell value before truncation.

watchBudgetMs.description: Shared per-stop time budget for evaluating watch expressions before remaining ones show a click-to-evaluate placeholder.

sourceMap.description: Glob patterns (e.g. "${workspaceFolder}/procs/**/*.sql") checked against each module's server definition (CRLF-normalized byte match); a match binds breakpoints and stack frames to that real file instead of the read-only tsqldbg: document.

logLevel.description: Debug Console verbosity. 'normal' (default) shows your script's output (PRINT, SELECT result sets), errors, logpoints, and commit/rollback notices. 'verbose' additionally shows the debugger's own diagnostic annotations: the NOCOUNT-forced-ON note, '-- GO: entering batch k of N', the untracked-SET-options note, and the DML-trigger heads-up. Distinct from 'trace' (the adapter-internal log file).

configurationSnippets.label: T-SQL: Debug Script
configurationSnippets.description: Debug the active .sql file — pick a connection at launch (Connection Manager).

configurationSnippets.label: T-SQL: Debug Stored Procedure
configurationSnippets.description: Debug a deployed procedure — pick a connection at launch (Connection Manager).
